task:
Onboarding hardening pass, Atom 1 — run identity & status clarity. CONTEXT: triggered by the second live Job Hunt onboarding (run_188 / Job Hunt run 1). Founder-facing surfaces must show the workspace-local display number ('Job Hunt run 1' / 'run 1') while keeping the global engine id as a technical id ('technical id: run_188') only where needed for debugging/paths/support.

OWNER-MAP (MANDATORY — DO NOT FORK): this EXTENDS the existing per-root display-number owner from Atom G / ticket 0028. The single owner is runDisplayName / coCoderRunReference / displayNumber in packages/core/src/store/portable/display.ts, plus the daemon run-display.ts accessor (withPortableDisplayNumber). Every remaining founder-facing emit-site must DERIVE from that owner — do NOT introduce a second labeling scheme, and do NOT remove run_188-style ids from file paths or internal storage. Before changing anything, grep the live tree to enumerate the COMPLETE set of founder-facing emit-sites; do not trust this list as exhaustive. Cover at minimum: UI + run detail, Deb status feed, Deb watch text, closeout & pickup language, founder-surfaced run records, and any Oz status summaries. For each site, verify it routes through the shared owner.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. Audit every founder-facing run-label emit-site (grep for run.id / `run_` usage in founder-facing strings across packages/daemon, packages/ui, packages/core). For each that shows a raw global id to the founder, route it through runDisplayName/coCoderRunReference so it renders the workspace display number ('run 1') with the technical id available as 'technical id: run_188' only where debugging/paths/support need it.
2. Fix Deb status projection so verify state is tied to the ACTIVE atom: if atom 1 is building and atom 0 passed, Deb must NOT show 'verify-1 pass'. Show verify idle for the active atom, or show 'last verified atom' separately. Handoffs must be atom-correct. Find the Deb status projection code and the place where verify state is attributed to an atom index.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- A run with global id run_188 + workspace display number 1 renders founder-facing as 'workspace run 1' (technical id shown only where debugging/paths need it). Add/extend a test proving this.
- Deb status does not attribute atom 0's verify pass to atom 1. Add/extend a test proving the active-atom verify state is correct (atom 1 building + atom 0 passed => no 'verify-1 pass').
- All founder-facing labels derive from the single display owner; no second labeling scheme introduced.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: do not remove run_NNN ids from file paths or internal storage/keys; ordinary (non-onboarding) runs' labels must keep working; the null/fallback-to-run.id behavior pinned by Atom G must remain.

SCOPE GUARD: CoCoder's own source/tests/templates/docs only — NO target product-code changes. Effective write scope: packages/** and cocoder docs as needed.

VERIFICATION: run the affected unit suites (runner status, Deb status projection, core display, daemon run-display) and report exact commands, exit codes, and relevant output. Add targeted tests where coverage is missing.

Atom 1 fix-up — your Atom 1 changes are correct and verified EXCEPT the @cocoder/core suite is red: two tests in packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts still assert the OLD 'Run N' label format that Atom 1 deliberately retired to 'workspace run N'. Keep all your production changes; do NOT revert the display-owner or status changes.

FIX:
1. packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts — the 'dispatches the wrap-up Play as a HEADLESS subprocess …' test asserts the pickup matches /Run \d+ on priority demo\./ . The pickup now renders via runDisplayName as 'workspace run N on priority demo'. Update that assertion to the new format (e.g. /workspace run \d+ on priority demo\./).
2. packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts — the 'visible Oscar wrap is delivered after landing outcome …' test asserts /WRAP-UP READY for Run \d+\./ . The delivery now renders 'WRAP-UP READY for workspace run N.'. Update that assertion to /WRAP-UP READY for workspace run \d+\./ .
3. Grep the WHOLE test tree for any other stale assertions on the old labels — search packages/**/tests and packages/**/*.test.* for: 'for Run ', 'as Run ', 'Run \\d', '# Run ', 'Run 1 ', '`Run ', and 'on priority demo' — and update every one the label change broke. Do not leave a second red hiding behind these two.

DO NOT change any production source for this fix unless a grep turns up a real second emit-site still printing the old 'Run N' wording to the founder (if so, route it through the shared owner, do not hardcode).

ACCEPTANCE: `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` is fully GREEN, and the daemon + ui suites you touched (`pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test`, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/ui test`) are GREEN. Report exact commands, exit codes, and the pass/fail tallies for each.

SCOPE: tests only (plus the named production fix only if a grep proves a missed emit-site). No target product code.

Onboarding hardening pass, Atom 2 — onboarding gates & scope. Two cohesive parts on the onboarding flow. The onboarding signal is the EXISTING `auditWriteBoundary` presence on a priority (do NOT add a new onboarding flag). Preserve ADR-0026: onboarding runs the ordinary Oscar/Bob/founder loop — do NOT build a parallel/standalone phase executor.

OWNER-MAP (verified file:line — fix the owner, don't copy):
- Priority frontmatter parse: packages/core/src/priorities/loader.ts (~L49-55) — parses `scopeNarrowing` and `auditWriteBoundary`.
- Effective scope: packages/core/src/write-scope/partition.ts:14-16 `effectiveScope(personaScope, priorityNarrowing)` — scopeNarrowing REPLACES base when present. Bob base writeScope is `[]` (packages/personas/base/bob.md:5). Computed at packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts:607.
- Out-of-scope flag: packages/core/src/commit-gate/gate.ts:85-87 (`out-of-scope-committed` event) — advisory. Hard audit boundary throw: gate.ts:67-73 (`AuditWriteBoundaryError`).
- Directive loop / delegate dispatch: runner.ts (~L1335 awaits directive-N.json) -> executeAgentStep in packages/core/src/runner/agent-step.ts (~L85-140) creates the workItem and dispatches Bob. `auditWriteBoundary` is already threaded into this path (used by the commit gate).
- Event store: packages/core/src/store/types.ts:117/123 (recordEvent/listEvents). Existing run statuses include `awaiting-founder` (store/types.ts:12) and RunnerPhase `awaiting-founder` (runner/status.ts:11).
- SSOT template (the ONLY copy; base dup removed run_44): templates/workspace-cocoder/cocoder/priorities/onboard-existing.md (has `auditWriteBoundary: ["cocoder/**"]`, no scopeNarrowing).

PART A — scope agreement.
Add `scopeNarrowing: ["cocoder/**"]` to the SSOT onboard-existing.md frontmatter so Bob's effective write scope EQUALS the hard `auditWriteBoundary: ["cocoder/**"]`. Result: valid audit output (e.g. cocoder/audit/recon.md) commits IN-SCOPE and is no longer recorded `out-of-scope-committed` merely because Bob's base scope was empty; auditWriteBoundary stays the hard boundary (product-path writes still REFUSED at gate.ts:67-73). Test: prove an onboarding atom that writes cocoder/audit/recon.md is NOT flagged out-of-scope, and that a product-path write is still hard-refused.

PART B — mechanical recon/spend founder gate (runner-enforced, persistent, retry-safe).
The gate must persist ACROSS the founder wrap beat: after recon, Oscar wraps to awaiting-founder; the deep-read happens in a LATER run with a FRESH per-run event stream. So the gate MUST read PERSISTENT repo state (the target working tree under cocoder/**), NOT the in-run event stream.
- Define the gate: for an onboarding priority (auditWriteBoundary present), before dispatching a `delegate` atom, the runner checks the target tree: if the recon artifact `cocoder/audit/recon.md` EXISTS (recon already done) AND a spend-approval checkpoint `cocoder/audit/spend-approval.json` does NOT exist (no recorded founder approval) => BLOCK the delegate. Otherwise proceed. This is retry-safe: a failed recon never commits recon.md, so recon retries are never blocked; and it is cross-run correct because both files persist in the repo.
- The spend-approval checkpoint lives at cocoder/audit/spend-approval.json (inside the cocoder/** audit boundary, so it is itself a governed artifact). Recognize it by presence + a minimal valid shape (e.g. an object with `approved: true`). The founder/Oz records approval by creating this file; document that path in the block message — do NOT build a daemon/UI approval button in this atom (note it as a possible follow-up).
- The BLOCK is a clear founder-facing rejection, e.g. 'recon complete; spend approval required before expensive read — record approval at cocoder/audit/spend-approval.json'. Record a run event for it and surface it so Oscar's recovery is to wrap/pause with the recon map + spend estimate + a POPULATED Founder Decision Needed (not None). Do not silently drop the atom and do not auto-approve.
- Insertion point: the delegate-dispatch path (runner.ts directive handling around the executeAgentStep call, or the top of executeAgentStep where auditWriteBoundary + store + workspace path are in scope). Return/propagate a 'blocked' outcome using existing result shapes; do NOT invent a parallel gate contract or phase executor.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK:
- Non-onboarding priorities (no auditWriteBoundary): NO gate, behavior unchanged. Prove with a test.
- The auditWriteBoundary hard refusal of product-code writes (gate.ts:67-73) stays intact.
- ADR-0026: ordinary Oscar/Bob/founder loop; the gate is a single check, not a new executor.

TESTS/PROOF (add where coverage is missing; report exact commands, exit codes, tallies):
1. Part A: onboarding atom writing cocoder/audit/recon.md is in-scope (no out-of-scope-committed event); product-path write still hard-refused.
2. Part B block: with recon.md committed and no spend-approval.json, a subsequent delegate is BLOCKED with the founder-facing message + recorded event.
3. Part B open: with spend-approval.json present (valid shape), the subsequent delegate PROCEEDS to dispatch.
4. Part B retry-safety: before recon.md exists, delegates (incl. a recon retry) are NOT blocked.
5. Non-onboarding priority: no gate, unaffected.
Run the affected unit suites: priorities/loader, write-scope/partition, commit-gate, runner, status, plus the nearest onboarding proof (scripts/proof-onboard-existing.mjs). Report results.

SCOPE: CoCoder's own source/tests/templates/docs only. Effective write scope packages/** + templates/** + cocoder docs. NO target product code.

Onboarding hardening pass, Atom 3 — first-workspace setup defaults & disclosure. Three cohesive parts across the daemon createWorkspace path and the UI add-workspace flow. SCOPE GUARD: CoCoder's own source/tests/templates/docs only — NO target product code. Preserve existing saved roles (do not silently downgrade any already-configured writable folder).

OWNER-MAP (verified file:line):
- Roles: packages/daemon/src/registry.ts — `WorkspaceRole = 'primary' | 'writable' | 'readonly'` (L11), folder validation requires a valid role (L69).
- createWorkspace handler: packages/daemon/src/routes.ts:772-818. It git-inits non-git roots (`initializedRepo`, L796-797), scaffolds cocoder/** (L798), seeds the root .gitignore ONLY when it git-inits (`seedStarterRootGitignore`, L800-801, constant STARTER_ROOT_GITIGNORE L53, writer L413-416), commits governance (L809), then baseline-commits the tree when `initializedRepo && receipt.committed` (`commitBaselineTree`, L810). Current 201 response (L817) returns only { ok, workspace, legacyHidden, governanceCommittedSha, governanceCommitted, governanceCommitError? } — it does NOT disclose the primary root, roots+roles, git-init, baseline commit, or the .gitignore written outside cocoder/.
- UI add-root default role: packages/ui/src/renderer/sections/Workspaces.tsx:64 — `addRoot` defaults a NEW non-primary root to 'writable' (`role: hasPrimary ? 'writable' : 'primary'`). The explicit role <select> is at L45.
- UI create call + result handling: packages/ui/src/renderer/App.tsx:463 (`createWorkspace(oz, id, folders)`) and the action-status message; the renderer client is packages/ui/src/renderer/live.ts:173; IPC types in packages/ui/src/main/ipc-contract.ts (RunSummary/WorkspaceRole etc.).

PART 1 — non-primary folders default to readonly.
Change Workspaces.tsx:64 so a newly added non-primary root defaults to 'readonly' (not 'writable'); 'writable' must require the explicit founder action via the existing role <select>. Existing saved roles MUST be preserved (addRoot only appends to ...editing.roots — confirm no existing role is rewritten anywhere in this flow; do not add any re-derivation that could downgrade Anthony/About or another saved writable folder). Add/extend a UI test proving a newly added non-primary root is readonly by default and that an existing writable root is unchanged.

PART 2 — first-run scaffold disclosure (daemon response + UI surface).
Enrich the createWorkspace 201 response (routes.ts:817) with a disclosure object stating: the primary root path; the added roots with their roles; whether git was initialized (`initializedRepo`); whether a baseline commit was created (the same condition as L810: initializedRepo && receipt.committed); and EVERY file written OUTSIDE cocoder/ (for a git-inited root that is the root .gitignore). Compute the outside-cocoder file list explicitly (the .gitignore path when seedStarterRootGitignore wrote it); cocoder/** writes are already covered by governanceCommitted. Then surface this disclosure in the UI add-workspace result (App.tsx action message / live.ts createWorkspace return) so the founder plainly sees primary root, roots+roles, git init y/n, baseline commit y/n, and any file written outside cocoder/. Update ipc-contract.ts types as needed. Tests: daemon test asserting the response discloses git-init + baseline + the outside-cocoder .gitignore for a non-git root, and discloses none-written-outside for an already-git root; UI test asserting the disclosure is shown.

PART 3 — root .gitignore: keep AND disclose.
Keep the seedStarterRootGitignore write (do NOT remove it), but it must be disclosed as a file written outside cocoder/ (Part 2's outside-cocoder list). The invariant: onboarding NEVER silently writes a target-repo file outside cocoder/ — every such write is disclosed in the create result. (No need to move it behind an option; disclosure satisfies the requirement.) Cover with the Part 2 daemon test.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: already-git roots get no .gitignore/baseline (unchanged) and disclose nothing-outside-cocoder; the governance commit receipt fields stay; existing saved workspace roles are never downgraded; non-onboarding behavior unchanged.

VERIFICATION: run the affected suites — daemon (workspace registration/registry, routes/createWorkspace, scaffold) and ui (Workspaces, live-app/adapter) — plus `node scripts/proof-nongit-onboard.mjs`. Report exact commands, exit codes, and pass/fail tallies. Add targeted tests where coverage is missing.
